name: Two Pointers (in-place)
summary: One pointer at each end. Skip past any non-alphanumeric character, then compare the two ends case-insensitively; step inward until they meet. No cleaned copy is built.
visualizationIntent: init: the full string shown as character cells (including spaces and punctuation); left pointer at 0, right at the last index. loop: while left < right. skip (L6-9): when an end character isn't alphanumeric, that pointer advances past it. check (L10): compare the two end characters case-insensitively (compared). done (L11): a mismatch flashes both ends error and returns false. move (L12-13): on a match, both pointers step inward and the matched ends lock visited. return (L14): the pointers crossed with no mismatch — palindrome (all result).

lineExplanations.3: Start a pointer at the first character.
lineExplanations.4: Start a pointer at the last character.
lineExplanations.5: Keep going while the two pointers haven't met.
lineExplanations.6: If the left character isn't alphanumeric, get ready to skip it.
lineExplanations.7: Move the left pointer past the non-alphanumeric character.
lineExplanations.8: If the right character isn't alphanumeric, get ready to skip it.
lineExplanations.9: Move the right pointer past the non-alphanumeric character.
lineExplanations.10: Compare the two end characters, ignoring letter case.
lineExplanations.11: They differ — the string is not a palindrome.
lineExplanations.12: They matched — move the left pointer inward.
lineExplanations.14: The pointers met with every compared pair matching — it's a palindrome.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: — the class wrapper the platform expects your method to live inside.
syntaxExplanations.2: def isPalindrome(self, s): — s is the raw string, punctuation and all.
syntaxExplanations.3: left = 0 — pointer at the first character.
syntaxExplanations.4: right = len(s) - 1 — pointer at the last character.
syntaxExplanations.5: while left < right: — keep comparing until the pointers meet.
syntaxExplanations.6: while left < right and not s[left].isalnum(): — skip a left character that isn't a letter or digit.
syntaxExplanations.7: left += 1 — advance left past the skipped character.
syntaxExplanations.8: while left < right and not s[right].isalnum(): — skip a right character that isn't a letter or digit.
syntaxExplanations.9: right -= 1 — advance right past the skipped character.
syntaxExplanations.10: if s[left].lower() != s[right].lower(): — compare the two ends, ignoring case.
syntaxExplanations.11: return False — the ends differ, so it's not a palindrome.
syntaxExplanations.12: left += 1 — the ends matched; move left inward.
syntaxExplanations.14: return True — the pointers met with every pair matching — it's a palindrome.
